question_title: What will the S&P 500 index close level be on April 30, 2026?
summary:
The S&P 500 closed at 6,740 on March 6, 2026, in the midst of a selloff driven by the US-Iran war (with no near-term resolution signals), spiking oil prices (calls for $100+), and a surprise -92K February NFP report. VIX is at 29.5, indicating extreme near-term volatility. The forecast horizon is ~39 trading days to April 30, 2026.

The distribution is centered near current levels (~6710 median), reflecting the tension between historical mean reversion from drawdowns (62.7% of similar 3%+ drawdown episodes see positive 39-day returns, median +2.28%) and the specific severity of current geopolitical and economic risks. The distribution is deliberately wide (80% CI: 5866-7468) due to the binary nature of the key uncertainty: whether the Iran conflict resolves (pushing toward 7200+) or escalates (pushing toward 6000 or below via Strait of Hormuz disruption). Monte Carlo simulation with 4 weighted scenarios (resolution 30%, prolonged conflict 40%, escalation 15%, quick resolution 15%) using VIX-implied volatility, blended with empirical conditional returns from historical drawdown episodes.

factors.description: Current S&P 500 at 6740, down 3.7% from 52-week high of 7002. Starting point anchors the distribution center near current level.
US-Iran war ongoing with no near-term resolution signals. Trump demands unconditional surrender, oil spiking with calls for $100+. Creates persistent downward pressure and elevated volatility.
VIX at 29.5 on March 6 - significantly elevated, indicating market expects continued turbulence. VIX-implied daily vol of 1.86% vs long-run ~1%.
Weak February jobs data: -92K nonfarm payrolls. Suggests economic slowdown, potentially stagflationary with rising oil prices.
Historical conditional returns from 3%+ drawdown episodes (n=485 since 1927): median 39-day forward return is +2.28%, 62.7% positive. Mean reversion tendency favors recovery.
factors.conditional: If current drawdown follows historical patterns

factors.description: Wall Street year-end 2026 targets range 7100-8000 (consensus ~7500). Pro-rated to April 30 suggests ~7000-7200. Long-term bullish view intact if war resolves.
2025 tariff crash precedent: markets crashed April 2025 then recovered within ~2 months. Provides some recovery template, but active military conflict is qualitatively different.
Strait of Hormuz disruption risk: handles 13M barrels/day. Full closure could push oil to $100-150, severely damaging global economy. Fat left tail risk with no clean historical analogue.
factors.conditional: If Strait of Hormuz is significantly disrupted

reasoning:
**S&P 500 Forecast for April 30, 2026**

| Percentile | Level | Change from Current |
|-----------|-------|-------------------|
| P10 | 5,866 | -13.0% |
| P20 | 6,181 | -8.3% |
| P40 | 6,556 | -2.7% |
| P60 | 6,863 | +1.8% |
| P80 | 7,214 | +7.0% |
| P90 | 7,468 | +10.8% |

**Key drivers of the wide distribution:**
- **Downside** (P10-P20): War escalation, Strait of Hormuz disruption, $100+ oil, recession
- **Center** (~6,710 implied median): Muddling through — conflict drags on, market trades sideways
- **Upside** (P80-P90): Conflict resolution, oil normalization, relief rally + earnings momentum

The distribution is centered near current levels (-0.5% median) with a wide 80% CI of ~1,600 points, reflecting the genuinely binary nature of the Iran conflict outcome over the next 39 trading days.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the numeric value of the S&P 500 (series SP500) for April 30, 2026 as shown on FRED before May 1, 2026, using https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/SP500
fine_print: If April 30, 2026 is not a US equity trading day and SP500 has no observation for that date, use the most recent prior trading day’s SP500 observation. Record the index level exactly as shown (do not apply additional rounding beyond the displayed value). Candidate Sources: S&P Dow Jones Indices S&P 500 index page (backup) https://www.spglobal.com/spdji/en/indices/equity/sp-500/ | Yahoo Finance S&P 500 index historical data (backup) https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/history
background_info:
This forecasts the level of the leading US equity benchmark, summarizing risk appetite and earnings expectations.
